sourceMovie.title: Lord of War
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Lord of War (2005) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Andrew Niccol's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Donnie Brasco
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lord of War clicked for you, Donnie Brasco (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lord of War?
faq.answer: Try Donnie Brasco, Carlito's Way, You Were Never Really Here—each shares DNA with Lord of War while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lord of War?
faq.answer: Lord of War (2005) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lord of War part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Lord of War Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
